steps.description: Audit and correct the backend upload environment and app wiring so the new file routes are registered exactly once, static local uploads are served in development, and route integration avoids the type/runtime issues reported in the previous failed run. Preserve hub-file safety by only making minimal adapter-style changes in the shared app bootstrap and upload config.

Fix the file upload route implementation so it actually supports the required contract without introducing new errors: validate request bodies and UUID params with Zod, enforce 1GB max size and blocked extensions, return correct local-vs-S3 upload metadata, add real multipart handling for local dev uploads, write files into uploads/, update file status to uploaded, and return metadata plus download URLs consistently.

Repair and complete the comments route implementation so all endpoints match the requested API shape and compile cleanly: grouped thread listing by comment_id, thread creation/reply creation, content updates, resolve endpoint setting resolved_at, delete authorization for author/admin, and consistent response envelopes/field naming expected by frontend consumers and tests.

Fix the backlinks route implementation to remove current compile/runtime issues and align with the requested behavior: validate UUID params, return source documents with title and type for backlinks, and upsert document_links from extracted mentions/links without broken variable references or malformed response shapes.

Update backend route tests to match the corrected API behavior and catch the regressions that caused the previous verification errors, including local multipart upload success, file metadata/download URL responses, grouped comments flows, resolve persistence, and backlink upsert/list behavior.

Repair the frontend upload service to match the backend contract and eliminate the type/interface mismatches introduced previously: normalize camelCase responses, support presigned S3 PUT uploads and local multipart POST uploads, expose requestUpload/uploadFile/trackUploadProgress with consistent types, and integrate with the upload tracker utilities without breaking existing editor consumers.

Align the upload navigation warning UI/context with the corrected tracker API so navigation blocking works without new type errors, and ensure the warning component consumes the tracker/context state shape consistently.

Fix the TanStack Query comments hook so it matches the backend response shape and endpoint methods exactly, supports fetch/create/reply/update/resolve/delete mutations, and avoids the schema mismatches introduced in the previous attempt.

+   const result = await pool.query(
+     `SELECT d.id, d.document_type AS document_type, d.title
+      FROM document_links dl
+      JOIN documents d ON dl.source_id = d.id
+      WHERE dl.target_id = $1
+        AND d.workspace_id = $2
+        AND d.deleted_at IS NULL
+        AND d.archived_at IS NULL
+      ORDER BY dl.created_at DESC, d.created_at DESC`,
+     [parsedId.data, workspaceId]
+   );
+ 
+   const backlinks = result.rows.map((row) => {
+     const parsed = documentLinksDbSchema.safeParse({ source_id: row.id, target_id: parsedId.data });
+     if (!parsed.success) {
+       return null;
+     }
+     return {
+       id: parsed.data.source_id,
+       title: row.title,
+       type: row.document_type,
+     };
+   }).filter((row): row is BacklinkSummary => row !== null);
+ 
+   return res.json(backlinks);"}

     3	test.describe('Inline Comments', () => {
     4	  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
     5	    await page.goto('/login')
     6	    await page.locator('#email').fill('dev@ship.local')
     7	    await page.locator('#password').fill('admin123')
     8	    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign in', exact: true }).click()
     9	    await expect(page).not.toHaveURL('/login', { timeout: 5000 })
    10	  })
    11	
    12	  /**
    13	   * Helper: create a document, type text, and return the document page
    14	   */
    15	  async function createDocumentWithText(page: any, text: string) {
    16	    await page.goto('/docs')
    17	    const newButton = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'New Document', exact: true })
    18	    await expect(newButton).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    19	    await newButton.click()
    20	    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/documents\/[a-f0-9-]+/, { timeout: 10000 })
    21	    await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    22	
    23	    // Type text into editor
    24	    const editor = page.locator('.ProseMirror')
    25	    await editor.click()
    26	    await page.keyboard.type(text, { delay: 5 })
    27	    // Wait for content to sync
    28	    await page.waitForTimeout(500)
    29	  }
    30	
    31	  /**
    32	   * Helper: select a specific substring within the editor paragraph
    33	   */
    34	  async function selectText(page: any, target: string) {
    35	    await page.evaluate((t: string) => {
    36	      const p = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tiptap-editor"] .ProseMirror p')
    37	      if (!p) return
    38	      const text = p.textContent || ''
    39	      const idx = text.indexOf(t)
    40	      if (idx === -1) return
    41	      const walker = document.createTreeWalker(p, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT)
    42	      let node: Text | null
    43	      let offset = 0
    44	      while ((node = walker.nextNode() as Text | null)) {
    45	        const len = node.textContent?.length || 0
    46	        if (offset + len > idx) {
    47	          const range = document.createRange()
    48	          range.setStart(node, idx - offset)
    49	          range.setEnd(node, idx - offset + t.length)
    50	          window.getSelection()?.removeAllRanges()
    51	          window.getSelection()?.addRange(range)
    52	          break
    53	        }
    54	        offset += len
    55	      }
    56	    }, target)
    57	    await page.waitForTimeout(400)
    58	  }
    59	
    60	  test('bubble menu shows Comment button on text selection', async ({ page }) => {
    61	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'Select this text to see the comment button appear.')
    62	
    63	    await selectText(page, 'this text')
    64	
    65	    // BubbleMenu should appear with Comment button
    66	    const commentBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Comment' })
    67	    await expect(commentBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
    68	  })
    69	
    70	  test('can create an inline comment via bubble menu', async ({ page }) => {
    71	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'This paragraph has text that will receive an inline comment.')
    72	
    73	    await selectText(page, 'inline comment')
    74	
    75	    // Click Comment in bubble menu
    76	    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Comment' }).click()
    77	
    78	    // Comment input should appear
    79	    const commentInput = page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Write a comment...' })
    80	    await expect(commentInput).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
    81	
    82	    // Type and submit
    83	    await commentInput.fill('This is a test comment')
    84	    await commentInput.press('Enter')
    85	
    86	    // Wait for API response and decoration re-render
    87	    await page.waitForTimeout(1500)
    88	
    89	    // The inline comment card should appear with the comment text
    90	    await expect(page.getByText('This is a test comment')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    91	
    92	    // The highlighted text should have the comment-highlight class
    93	    const highlight = page.locator('.comment-highlight')
    94	    await expect(highlight).toBeVisible()
    95	  })
    96	
    97	  test('can create a comment via Cmd+Shift+M keyboard shortcut', async ({ page }) => {
    98	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'Testing keyboard shortcut for adding comments quickly.')
    99	
   100	    await selectText(page, 'keyboard shortcut')
   101	
   102	    // Press Cmd+Shift+M
   103	    await page.keyboard.press('Meta+Shift+m')
   104	
   105	    // Comment input should appear
   106	    const commentInput = page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Write a comment...' })
   107	    await expect(commentInput).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
   108	
   109	    // Submit comment
   110	    await commentInput.fill('Created via keyboard shortcut')
   111	    await commentInput.press('Enter')
   112	
   113	    await page.waitForTimeout(1500)
   114	
   115	    await expect(page.getByText('Created via keyboard shortcut')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
   116	  })
   117	
   118	  test('canceling a comment removes the highlight', async ({ page }) => {
   119	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'This text will have a comment that gets canceled.')
   120	
   121	    await selectText(page, 'comment that gets canceled')
   122	
   123	    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Comment' }).click()
   124	
   125	    const commentInput = page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Write a comment...' })
   126	    await expect(commentInput).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
   127	
   128	    // Press Escape to cancel
   129	    await page.keyboard.press('Escape')
   130	
   131	    // Highlight should be removed (auto-retries until timeout)
   132	    await expect(page.locator('.comment-highlight')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 })
   133	  })
   134	
   135	  test('inline comment card shows quoted text, author, and timestamp', async ({ page }) => {
   136	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'The quoted text should appear in the comment card.')
   137	
   138	    await selectText(page, 'quoted text')
   139	
   140	    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Comment' }).click()

   142	    await commentInput.fill('Checking the card layout')
   143	    await commentInput.press('Enter')
   144	
   145	    await page.waitForTimeout(1500)
   146	
   147	    // Verify card shows quoted text
   148	    await expect(page.getByText('"quoted text"')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
   149	
   150	    // Verify author name
   151	    await expect(page.locator('.comment-author').first()).toContainText('Dev User')
   152	
   153	    // Verify timestamp
   154	    await expect(page.locator('.comment-time').first()).toBeVisible()
   155	
   156	    // Verify reply input
   157	    const replyInput = page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Reply...' })
   158	    await expect(replyInput).toBeVisible()
   159	  })
   160	
   161	  test('can reply to an existing comment', async ({ page }) => {
   162	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'This comment will receive a reply from another user.')
   163	
   164	    await selectText(page, 'receive a reply')

   172	
   173	    // Click the reply input and type a reply
   174	    const replyInput = page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Reply...' })
   175	    await replyInput.click()
   176	    await page.keyboard.type('This is a reply to the original', { delay: 5 })
   177	    await page.keyboard.press('Enter')
   178	    await page.waitForTimeout(1500)
   179	
   180	    // Reload to verify persistence
   181	    await page.reload()
   182	    await page.waitForTimeout(3000)
   183	
   184	    // Both comments should be visible after reload
   185	    await expect(page.getByText('Original comment')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
   186	    await expect(page.getByText('This is a reply to the original')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
   187	  })
   188	
   189	  test('resolving a comment collapses thread and removes highlight', async ({ page }) => {
   190	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'This highlighted text will be resolved and collapsed.')
   191

  "scripts": {
    "dev": "./scripts/dev.sh",
    "docker:up": "docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml up --build",
    "docker:down": "docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml down",
    "docker:clean": "docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml down -v",
    "dev:raw": "pnpm --parallel --recursive run dev",
    "dev:api": "pnpm --filter @ship/api dev",
    "dev:web": "pnpm --filter @ship/web dev",
    "dev:shared": "pnpm --filter @ship/shared dev",
    "build": "pnpm --recursive run build",
    "build:shared": "pnpm --filter @ship/shared build",
    "build:api": "pnpm run build:shared && pnpm --filter @ship/api build",
    "build:web": "pnpm run build:shared && pnpm --filter @ship/web build",
    "demo:proof": "./scripts/run-demo-proof.sh",
    "demo:proof:full": "./scripts/run-demo-proof.sh --full-gate",
    "demo:proof:a11y": "./scripts/run-demo-proof.sh --with-a11y",
    "smoke:hostinger": "./scripts/smoke-hostinger.sh",
    "type-check": "pnpm --recursive run type-check",
    "lint": "pnpm --recursive run lint",
    "clean": "pnpm --recursive run clean && rm -rf node_modules",
    "test": "pnpm --filter @ship/api test",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "test:e2e:isolated": "playwright test --config=playwright.isolated.config.ts",
    "test:e2e:preflight": "node scripts/playwright-preflight.mjs",
    "test:e2e:ui": "playwright test --ui",
    "track:agent-usage": "node scripts/track-agent-usage.js",
    "worktree:init": "./scripts/worktree-init.sh",
    "db:seed": "pnpm --filter @ship/api db:seed",
    "db:migrate": "pnpm --filter @ship/api db:migrate",
    "db:orphan-check": "pnpm --filter @ship/api db:orphan-check",
    "loadtest": "pnpm --filter @ship/api loadtest:task-0",
    "loadtest:task-0": "pnpm --filter @ship/api loadtest:task-0",
    "loadtest:task-0:smoke": "pnpm --filter @ship/api loadtest:task-0:smoke",
    "loadtest:task-0:standard": "pnpm --filter @ship/api loadtest:task-0:standard",
    "loadtest:task-0:extended": "pnpm --filter @ship/api loadtest:task-0:extended",
    "loadtest:task-2": "pnpm --filter @ship/api loadtest:task-2",
    "loadtest:task-2:smoke": "pnpm --filter @ship/api loadtest:task-2:smoke",
    "loadtest:task-2:standard": "pnpm --filter @ship/api loadtest:task-2:standard",
    "loadtest:task-2:extended": "pnpm --filter @ship/api loadtest:task-2:extended",
    "prepare": "husky",
    "postinstall": "git config merge.ours.driver true && (command -v comply >/dev/null 2>&1 || echo '\\n⚠️  comply CLI not installed - pre-commit hooks require it\\n   Install with: pip install comply-cli\\n')"
  },

    "@axe-core/playwright": "^4.11.0",

    "@types/supertest": "^6.0.3",
    "@vitest/ui": "^4.0.16",
    "get-port": "^7.1.0",
    "husky": "^9.1.7",
    "prettier": "^3.4.2",
    "supertest": "^7.1.4",
    "testcontainers": "^11.11.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.2",
    "vitest": "^4.0.16"
  }
}

    12	
    13	  /**
    14	   * Helper: create a document, type text, and return the document page
    15	   */
    16	  async function createDocumentWithText(page: any, text: string) {
    17	    await page.goto('/docs')
    18	    const newButton = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'New Document', exact: true })
    19	    await expect(newButton).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    20	    await newButton.click()
    21	    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/documents\/[a-f0-9-]+/, { timeout: 10000 })
    22	    await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    23	
    24	    // Type text into editor
    25	    const editor = page.locator('.ProseMirror')
    26	    await editor.click()
    27	    await page.keyboard.type(text, { delay: 5 })
    28	    // Wait for content to sync
    29	    await page.waitForTimeout(500)
    30	  }
    31	
    32	  /**
    33	   * Helper: select a specific substring within the editor paragraph
    34	   */
    35	  async function selectText(page: any, target: string) {
    36	    await page.evaluate((t: string) => {
    37	      const p = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tiptap-editor"] .ProseMirror p')
    38	      if (!p) return
    39	      const text = p.textContent || ''
    40	      const idx = text.indexOf(t)
    41	      if (idx === -1) return
    42	      const walker = document.createTreeWalker(p, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT)
    43	      let node: Text | null
    44	      let offset = 0
    45	      while ((node = walker.nextNode() as Text | null)) {
    46	        const len = node.textContent?.length || 0
    47	        if (offset + len > idx) {
    48	          const range = document.createRange()
    49	          range.setStart(node, idx - offset)
    50	          range.setEnd(node, idx - offset + t.length)
    51	          window.getSelection()?.removeAllRanges()
    52	          window.getSelection()?.addRange(range)
    53	          break
    54	        }
    55	        offset += len
    56	      }
    57	    }, target)
    58	    await page.waitForTimeout(400)
    59	  }
    60	
    61	  test('bubble menu shows Comment button on text selection', async ({ page }) => {
    62	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'Select this text to see the comment button appear.')
    63	
    64	    await selectText(page, 'this text')
    65	
    66	    // BubbleMenu should appear with Comment button
    67	    const commentBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Comment' })
    68	    await expect(commentBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
    69	  })
    70	
    71	  test('can create an inline comment via bubble menu', async ({ page }) => {
    72	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'This paragraph has text that will receive an inline comment.')
    73	
    74	    await selectText(page, 'inline comment')
    75	
    76	    // Click Comment in bubble menu
    77	    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Comment' }).click()
    78	
    79	    // Comment input should appear
    80	    const commentInput = page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Write a comment...' })
    81	    await expect(commentInput).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
    82	
    83	    // Type and submit
    84	    await commentInput.fill('This is a test comment')
    85	    await commentInput.press('Enter')
    86	
    87	    // Wait for API response and decoration re-render
    88	    await page.waitForTimeout(1500)
    89	
    90	    // The inline comment card should appear with the comment text
    91	    await expect(page.getByText('This is a test comment')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    92	
    93	    // The highlighted text should have the comment-highlight class

   118

   121

   123

   126	    const commentInput = page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Write a comment...' })

   128	
   129	    // Press Escape to cancel

   131	
   132	    // Highlight should be removed (auto-retries until timeout)

   134	  })
   135	
   136	  test('inline comment card shows quoted text, author, and timestamp', async ({ page }) => {
   137	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'The quoted text should appear in the comment card.')
   138

   161	
   162	  test('can reply to an existing comment', async ({ page }) => {

messages.content:
Implement file upload and inline comments systems for Ship.

For file uploads, create `routes/files.ts` with auth middleware: POST /api/files/upload-request (validates filename/mimeType/sizeBytes with Zod, blocks dangerous extensions like .exe/.bat/.sh/.dll/.jar/.ps1, creates a files row with status=pending, returns a presigned S3 upload URL if S3_UPLOADS_BUCKET is configured or a local upload endpoint for dev), POST /api/files/upload/:id (for local dev: accepts multipart form data, writes to uploads/ directory, updates files row to status=uploaded), GET /api/files/:id (returns file metadata + download URL). For S3: use @aws-sdk/client-s3 and @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner with 15-minute URL expiration. For local dev: serve uploads/ as static files. Set max file size to 1GB. Validate UUID format on all ID params to prevent path traversal.

For comments, create `routes/comments.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/comments (list all comment threads for a document, grouped by comment_id), POST /api/documents/:id/comments (create a new comment thread with comment_id matching the TipTap comment mark, or reply to existing via parent_id), PATCH /api/comments/:id (update comment content), POST /api/comments/:id/resolve (set resolved_at timestamp), DELETE /api/comments/:id (only author or admin can delete). Create `routes/backlinks.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks (query document_links where target_id matches, return source documents with title and type), POST /api/documents/:id/links (upsert document_links from extracted mentions/links in content).

On the frontend, create `src/services/upload.ts` with: requestUpload (calls upload-request endpoint), uploadFile (either uploads directly to presigned S3 URL or to the local endpoint), trackUploadProgress. Create `src/services/uploadTracker.ts` to track active uploads and warn on navigation (UploadNavigationWarning component). Create `src/hooks/useCommentsQuery.ts` for fetching and mutating comments via TanStack Query.

Verify: upload an image via the API, confirm it returns a valid URL, embed it in a TipTap document via the editor, confirm it renders. Create a comment thread on a document, reply to it, resolve it, confirm the resolved_at timestamp is set.

<plan>
[
  {
    "index": 0,
    "description": "Fix the shared upload environment configuration via the hub-file-safe adapter pattern so file routes can reliably determine local vs S3 behavior, upload limits, upload URL expiry, and upload/static paths without breaking downstream env consumers. Because env.ts is imported by more than 8 files, do not rewrite its exported surface directly; add a new implementation file and make env.ts re-export the corrected implementation while preserving existing exports.",
    "files": [
      "/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored/api/src/config/env.ts",
      "/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored/api/src/config/env-v2.ts"
    ]
  },
  {
    "index": 1,
    "description": "Bring the backend file upload routes into full compliance with the requested API contract: validate request payloads and UUID params with Zod, enforce the 1GB cap and dangerous-extension blocklist, support S3 presigned PUT uploads with 15-minute expiry, support local multipart uploads that write into uploads/, and return metadata plus download URLs from GET /api/files/:id. Adjust local static registration behavior if needed so uploads are correctly served in dev.",
    "files": [
      "/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored/api/src/routes/files.ts",
      "/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored/api/src/app.ts"
    ]
  },
  {
    "index": 2,
    "description": "Align the backend comments routes with the required inline comment thread model: GET /api/documents/:id/comments grouped by comment_id, POST /api/documents/:id/comments supporting root thread creation using the TipTap comment_id and replies via parent_id, PATCH /api/comments/:id for content edits, POST /api/comments/:id/resolve to stamp resolved_at, and DELETE /api/comments/:id restricted to author or admin. Ensure all document/comment ID params are UUID-validated and response shapes are consistent for frontend consumption.",
    "files": [
      "/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored/api/src/routes/comments.ts"
    ]
  },
  {
    "index": 3,
    "description": "Verify and, where necessary, adjust backlinks route behavior to exactly match the requested API contract: GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks returning source documents with title and type for target_id matches, and POST /api/documents/:id/links upserting document_links from extracted mentions/links payloads with UUID validation and workspace scoping.",
    "files": [
      "/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored/api/src/routes/backlinks.ts"
    ]
  },
  {
    "index": 4,
    "description": "Update backend OpenAPI schema registrations so the generated API documentation matches the implemented file, comment, and backlink endpoints, payloads, and response shapes after the route fixes.
